prestazioni: Engine swap to a Porsche 911 3.2L Type 930/20 flat-six (engine code: 930/20) with a custom-built air-cooled turbo system featuring a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger, custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust with Burns Stainless merge collectors, and a custom-mapped Bosch Motronic M2.1 ECU adapted for air-cooled applications via a specialized adapter harness from K-Box Systems.

Full drivetrain reinforcement with a Porsche 915/16 5-speed manual transmission (from a 911 SC) paired with a Guard Industries limited-slip differential (LSD) with 40% lockup, upgraded CV joints from Porsche 930 Turbo, and a custom lightweight flywheel from Tilton Engineering.

Suspension overhaul with a custom-fabricated front and rear subframe reinforcement using T45 steel tubing, paired with Öhlins DFV Road & Track coilovers (custom valved for the 356 chassis), adjustable spherical bearing control arms from Elephant Racing, and a custom-fabricated front sway bar from Hotchkis Motorsport (22mm solid, adjustable).
estetica: Full custom widebody conversion using a RWB-inspired hand-fabricated steel fender flares (front: +3.5 inches, rear: +4.5 inches) with integrated side skirts and a custom rear diffuser from 911 Speedster Design, all finished in a custom-mixed Porsche GT3 RS Lava Orange paint by Glasurit.

Custom lightweight aluminum hood with a functional central scoop for turbocharger intake, fabricated by The Metal Surgeon, paired with a custom front splitter from 911R Design and a rear ducktail spoiler from Porsche Classic (part #901-506-011-00) modified for aggressive rake.

Interior stripped and rebuilt with a full custom roll cage from Safety Devices (FIA-spec, 6-point, TIG-welded), Recaro SRD bucket seats (Porsche 964 mounting brackets adapted), a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (350mm, suede), and a custom aluminum dashboard with Smiths retro-styled gauges (boost, oil temp, oil pressure, wideband AFR) from Speedhut.
